title: Paths, File Admission, Root Boundaries, and Size Budgets · Guided Program Studio
summary: A library-tools pair incrementally builds bounded file-admission policy from an original fixed text fixture while retaining earlier Git, Python, Unicode, determinism, bounded-resource, accessibility, testing, and evidence requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded file-admission policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Accept PathLike input, preserve the user-facing path, and distinguish lexical path composition from filesystem resolution.
Verify existence, regular-file type, readability, and race-aware open behavior before analysis.
Enforce an explicit allowed-root and symlink policy using resolved ancestry evidence rather than prefix strings.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pathlike Contract
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Bookbot team handles case 8464 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to admit a regular file inside an allowed root under an explicit byte budget; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pathlike Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for accept pathlike input, preserve the user-facing path, and distinguish lexical path composition from filesystem resolution. For Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pathlike Contract, keep Pathlike Contract connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop File Identity
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-file-identity-1asjx3" that uses case 4223 to admit a regular file inside an allowed root under an explicit byte budget. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop File Identity.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns File Identity into observable performance evidence. For Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop File Identity, keep File Identity connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-file-identity-1asjx3
# Competency: bookbot-file-identity.
# Case 4223: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def evidence_bookbot_file_identity_1asjx3(path: str = "fixtures/story.txt", regular_file: bool = True, within_root: bool = True, bytes_read: int = 4096, byte_limit: int = 65536) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1asjx3 = "1asjx3-3xjsa1-4223"
    assert evidence_variant_1asjx3
    evidence_axes_1asjx3 = (
        "corpus1asjx3",
        "policy3xjsa1",
        "revision4223",
        "command1asjx34223",
        "report3xjsa14223",
        "test42231asjx3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1asjx3)) == 6
    if not path.strip() or byte_limit <= 0:
        return False, bytes_read, "path-policy"
    if not regular_file:
        return False, bytes_read, "file-type"
    if not within_root:
        return False, bytes_read, "root-escape"
    if bytes_read < 0 or bytes_read > byte_limit:
        return False, bytes_read, "size-budget"
    assert 0 <= bytes_read <= byte_limit
    return True, bytes_read, "admitted-file"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The File Identity structural check is only one gate. bounded file-admission policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

Build deterministic Python evidence to admit a regular file inside an allowed root under an explicit byte budget; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record stable path, size, modification observation, digest purpose, and open-file identity limits for reproducible analysis.

For Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop File Identity, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A filename uniquely identifies unchanged content over time.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop File Identity
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Bookbot team handles case 3416 during a large-file migration rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to admit a regular file inside an allowed root under an explicit byte budget; retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity; then introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record stable path, size, modification observation, digest purpose, and open-file identity limits for reproducible analysis.
